note:
使测量二进制大小变得如此困难的原因之一是，编译器可以在幕后执行很多可能导致二进制大小不同的工作，这也是为什么，尽管将其用作SnakeQR的粗略度量，我并不认为它是一个比较度量。对我们来说幸运的是，我们再也不用太担心二进制文件的大小了。不过，让我们来比较一下，只是为了好玩。
我们将比较clang 8.0.1和clang 10.0.0。因为我在这台机器上有编译器，所以让我们添加GCC 4.2.1、GCC 8.3.0、GCC 11.0.0.20200723、PCC1.2.0开发人员20200630和LACC0.0.1。这应该会给我们带来广泛的比较，尽管这再一次不应该被认为是科学的。1我们将使用十六进制的总大小作为我们的比较器。
note_en:
One of the things that make measuring binary sizes so difficult (and why, despite using it as a rough metric for  SnakeQR, I don't consider it a comparative metric) is that your compiler can do a lot behind the scenes that can cause binary sizes to differ. Fortunately for us, we no longer have to worry much about the size of our binaries. But let's compare things just for fun.
We will compare clang 8.0.1 to clang 10.0.0. And because I have the compilers on this machine, let's add in gcc 4.2.1, gcc 8.3.0, gcc 11.0.0.20200723,  pcc 1.2.0 DEVEL 20200630, and  lacc 0.0.1. That should give us a wide array of comparisons, though again this should not be considered scientific in any way.  1 We will use total size in hex as our comparator.
title: 编译器编号，以及为什么它们无关紧要
title_en: Compiler numbers, and why they don't matter

note:
特朗普政府和宽带行业重新开始反对加州的网络中立法，美国司法部(US Department Of Justice)和互联网服务提供商(ISP)游说团体昨日对该州提出了新的申诉。
此案已有近两年的历史，但由于加州在2018年10月同意暂停执行法律，直到联邦通信委员会(Federal Communications Commission)废除美国网络中立规则和联邦通信委员会(FCC)试图先发制人的诉讼结束后，该案被搁置。这起诉讼是在2019年10月做出裁决的，当时美国哥伦比亚特区巡回上诉法院(US Court Of Appeals For The District Of Columbia Circuit)维持了FCC对其自身规则的废除，但推翻了FCC试图在全国范围内对任何州的网络中立法实施全面优先购买权的企图。
从根本上说，委员会缺乏明确废除所有50个州的法律权威，法定授予的权力监管州内通信，该案的法官写道。但这并不能阻止特朗普政府和互联网服务提供商在个案的基础上试图阻止各州的法律。
FCC案件的上诉窗口上个月关闭，允许加州案件在美国加州东区地区法院推进。现在特朗普政府和宽带行业已经提交了修改后的投诉，加州必须在9月16日之前提交支持其网络中立法的简报。
加利福尼亚州的网络中立法禁止互联网服务提供商阻止或限制合法流量，并禁止向网站或在线服务收取费用，以向消费者提供流量或确定流量的优先顺序。该州法律还禁止付费数据上限豁免(所谓的零评级)，并表示互联网服务提供商不得试图通过降低网络互联点的流量来逃避网络中立保护。
尽管联邦通信委员会的优先购买令在法庭上被推翻，但司法部昨天修改后的起诉书辩称，加利福尼亚州的网络中立法被联邦法律先发制人，因此违反了美国宪法的至上条款。
然而，美国司法部表示，加利福尼亚州试图通过颁布SB-822[州网络中立法]来猜测联邦政府的监管方式。美国司法部表示，该州的法律还违反了各州和联邦政府之间的监管权力划分。在这种划分中，联邦通信委员会(FCC)独家负责监管州际通信，而各州则对州内通信负有责任，美国司法部表示，这项法律还违反了各州和联邦政府之间的监管权划分。根据这一划分，联邦通信委员会对监管州际通信负有独家责任，而各州则对州内通信负有责任。美国司法部表示，加州的法律违反了这一划分，因为互联网通信本质上是跨州的。
司法部还要求发布初步禁令，在案件悬而未决期间阻止加州法律的执行。
宽带行业修改后的申诉是由代表有线电视、光纤、DSL和移动互联网提供商的主要游说团体提出的。这个案例是违反宪法的州法规的一个经典例子。ISP游说团体写道，这项法规的目的是通过对宽带-州际通信服务-施加与联邦通信委员会(Federal Communications Commission)在2018年恢复互联网自由秩序(并采取更严格的法规)中明确废除的法规完全相同的规定，来废除和破坏联邦法律。#34；宽带是一项州际通信服务，联邦通信委员会在2018年恢复互联网自由秩序(并采取更严格的法规)中明确废除了同样的法规。
尽管联邦通信委员会在全国范围内的优先购买权已经被华盛顿特区巡回上诉法院驳回，但互联网服务提供商表示，法院之所以这样做，只是因为它发现联邦通信委员会没有权力明确先发制人，无法明确地先发制人，而且法院认识到，一些州的法律可能会与联邦通信委员会的优先购买令相冲突，并被其先发制人。此外，互联网服务提供商写道，法院的裁决没有涉及通信法本身是否先发制人，是否先发制人。
司法部辩称，重要的是，华盛顿特区巡回法庭没有决定[FCC废除]令的剩余部分是否根据冲突优先原则或任何其他默示优先原则具有先发制人的效果，并指出，冲突分析取决于我们掌握任何所谓冲突的事实。法院将该问题留到另一起案件中--就像这里--委员会做出……。特定于条款的参数。&#34；
加利福尼亚州可能会指出DC巡回命令的其他部分，该命令发现FCC的抢先权力是有限的，因为委员会放弃了其在宽带问题上的第二条监管权力。在委员会没有监管权力的任何领域，它同样没有先发制人的权力，法官在该案中写道。法官们写道，联邦通信委员会肯定的监管权力来源来自“通信法”第二、第三和第六章。但PAI&#39；的联邦通信委员会选择将标题I应用于宽带，而宽带并不包含这样的授权。
加州总检察长泽维尔·贝塞拉(Xavier Becera)的一位发言人告诉Ars，我们正在审查这些投诉，并期待着为加州的州网络中立保护辩护。
联邦通信委员会委员杰西卡·罗森沃塞尔(Jessica Rosenworcel)是一名民主党人，曾在奥巴马政府期间投票实施网络中立规则，后来反对主席阿吉特·派(Ajit Pai)的废除。她称司法部的诉讼是可耻的。
昨天，罗森沃塞尔在推特上写道，数百万人写信给联邦通信委员会，表示他们支持网络中立。但是联邦通信委员会没有听从。它不顾我的反对，取消了开放互联网的政策。因此，各州开始在自己的法律中纠正FCC犯下的错误。今天，美国司法部走上法庭，阻止加州的努力。可耻。&#34；
title: 特朗普政府对FCC在法庭上的失利不屑一顾，与加州作战。网络中立法

note:
Pixel4已经死了。这是在人们注意到谷歌商店将这款手机列为缺货后，谷歌向Verge发出的官方确认。Pixel 4于2019年10月24日开始发货，所以它的有效期只有九个半月。
谷歌的一位发言人向The Verge证实：“谷歌商店已经售出了Pixel4[和]4XL的库存并完成了销售。”“对于那些仍然有兴趣购买Pixel4[和]4XL的人来说，有些合作伙伴可以提供该产品，而供货可以持续。就像所有Pixel设备一样，Pixel 4将在至少三年内继续获得软件和安全更新，从该设备首次在美国的谷歌商店上市之日起算。“。
Pixel4的寿命是所有Pixel手机中最短的，仅为其他高端机型的一半。Pixel1、Pixel2和Pixel3的售价都在18个月左右。中档Pixel 3a的使用寿命也同样很短，在销售约13个月后就停产了。
这两次早期的停产都开启了谷歌一次退出智能手机市场长达数月的令人担忧的趋势。Pixel4的继任者Pixel5还没有发布，甚至还没有确定的发布日期。目前，对谷歌何时重返高端智能手机市场的最佳猜测是10月8日，这是谷歌法国泄露的Pixel 5预购日期。这将使谷歌在两个月内退出高端智能手机市场。谷歌在Pixel3a和4a之间也有一个月的销售差距。我想不出还有哪家公司会同意停售一两个月。
考虑到它有史以来最短的生命周期，手机的普遍接受度，以及发布后的内部影响，我认为我们可以正式称Pixel4为有史以来最糟糕的Pixel。售价800美元的Pixel4和售价900美元的Pixel4XL经历了艰难的生活。这些手机是围绕谷歌的试验性雷达手势系统而设计的，该项目的目标是为一款拥有巨大触摸屏的设备启用空中手势系统，这是一个值得怀疑的目标。空中手势系统从来没有真正可靠地工作过，但即使它们工作可靠，这些手势也做不了太多事情，只允许音乐控制、使警报静音和接听电话。Project Soli最初定位为智能手表的输入解决方案，可以跟踪和使用微妙的手指动作，而不是小屏幕。谷歌出货的是一款智能手机，它需要挥舞手臂的大手势，而大约6英寸的触摸屏使这些手势变得多余。
还有很多其他的问题。Pixel4是谷歌首次尝试3D人脸解锁系统，其版本比指纹扫描仪还慢。很多手机都觉得谷歌试图通过降低手机的规格来提高利润率，与价格相近的手机相比，这是一个很差的价值主张。与类似价格的竞争对手相比，它使用的存储速度更慢。它的内存较少。它的摄像头更少，出货的主摄像头硬件与Pixel2相同，也没有指纹识别器。它跳过了其SoC提供的Wi-Fi 6支持。与之前的Pixels相比，谷歌减少了手机提供的免费云存储选项。最糟糕的是，电池真的很小。实际上，唯一的亮点是90 Hz的显示屏，但由于电池问题，即使这样也受到限制，不能一直运行在90 Hz模式下。
据报道，谷歌对Pixel4也不满意。据“信息报”报道，谷歌硬件部门负责人里克·奥斯特罗在手机发布前向Pixel团队发表了讲话，报道称他不同意关于这款手机的一些决定，尤其是他对其电池电量感到失望。在Pixel4的内部和外部接收效果不佳之后，有两位高管离开了Pixel团队：Pixel总经理马里奥·奎罗兹和Pixel&，这两位高管分别是Pixel 4的总经理马里奥·奎罗斯(Mario Queiroz)和Pixel&，他们是：Pixel总经理马里奥·奎罗兹(Mario Queiroz)和Pixel&，这两位高管分别离开了Pixel团队：Pixel总经理马里奥·奎罗斯(Mario Queiroz)和像素&。列维的离开尤其刺痛，因为他的团队的相机软件魔法是Pixel系列的主要亮点。
随着Pixel4a的推出，不清楚为什么会有人选择800美元的Pixel4而不是350美元的Pixel4a。4a的设计更好，相同的主摄像头，更大的电池，相同的存储量和RAM。现在人们不再需要选择了。
note_en:
The Pixel 4 is dead. That's the official confirmation that Google sent to  The Verge after people noticed the Google Store listed the phone as "out of stock." The Pixel 4 started shipping October 24, 2019, so it was available for only nine-and-a-half months.
  "Google Store has sold through its inventory and completed sales of Pixel 4 [and] 4 XL,” a Google spokesperson confirms to  The Verge. “For people who are still interested in buying Pixel 4 [and] 4 XL, the product is available from some partners while supplies last. Just like all Pixel devices, Pixel 4 will continue to get software and security updates for at least three years from when the device first became available on the Google Store in the US.”
 The Pixel 4 had the shortest life span of any Pixel phone, only lasting half as long as the other high-end models. The Pixel 1, 2, and 3 were all for sale for around 18 months. The midrange Pixel 3a, had a  similarly short lifespan and was discontinued after about 13 months of sales.
 Both early discontinuations are starting the alarming trend of Google exiting segments of the smartphone market for months at a time. The Pixel 4's successor, the Pixel 5, is not out yet and doesn't even have a firm announcement date. Right now, the best guess for when Google will return to the high-end smartphone market is October 8, a date leaked by  Google France as the Pixel 5 pre-order date. That would put Google out of the high-end smartphone market for two months. Google also had a one-month sales gap between the Pixel 3a and 4a. I can't imagine any other business being OK with shutting down sales for a month or two.
  Due to its shortest-ever life cycle, the general reception of the phone, and the internal fallout after the device's launch, I think we can officially call the Pixel 4 "the worst Pixel ever." The $800 Pixel 4 and $900 Pixel 4 XL  had a rough life. The phones were designed around Google's experimental "Project Soli" radar gesture system, which had the questionable goal of enabling an air-gesture system for a device with a giant touchscreen. The air gesture system never really worked reliably, but even if they did, the gestures didn't do much, only allowing for music control, silencing alarms, and answering calls. Project Soli was  originally pitched as an input solution for smartwatches, where delicate finger movements could be tracked and used instead of the tiny screen. What Google shipped was a smartphone that required big arm-waving gestures that were made redundant by the ~6-inch touchscreen.
 There were many other problems. The Pixel 4 was Google's first swing at a 3D face unlock system, and its version was slower than a fingerprint scanner. A lot of the phone felt like Google was trying to increase its profit margins by cutting down the phone's specs, and it was  a poor value proposition compared to phones at similar prices. It used slower storage than the similarly priced competition. It had less RAM. It had fewer cameras and was shipping the same dated main camera hardware as the Pixel 2. It didn't have a fingerprint reader. It skipped out on the Wi-Fi 6 support that was offered by its SoC. Google cut down on the free cloud storage options offered with the phone compared to previous Pixels. Worst of all, the batteries were really tiny. Really the only highlight was the 90Hz display, but even that was limited to not run in 90Hz mode all the time, due to battery concerns.
 Google reportedly  was not happy with the Pixel 4, either. According to a report from The Information, the head of Google Hardware, Rick Osterloh addressed the Pixel team before the phone's launch, with the report saying he "did not agree with some of the decisions made about the phone" and "in particular, he was disappointed in its battery power." Following the poor internal and external reception of the Pixel 4, two top executives left the Pixel team: Pixel General Manager Mario Queiroz and the Pixel's camera lead, Marc Levoy. Levoy's departure particularly stings since his team's camera software wizardry was the main bright spot of the Pixel line.
 With the  launch of the Pixel 4a, it wasn't clear why anyone would pick the $800 Pixel 4 over the $350 Pixel 4a. The 4a has a better design, the same main camera, a bigger battery, and the same amount of storage and RAM. Now people won't have to choose.
title: 谷歌在销售不到一年后就停止销售Pixel4
title_en: Google discontinues the Pixel 4 after less than a year of sales

One person who has been pondering that question and providing answers  very successfully is  Max Levchin, and we’re very excited to have him as our special guest today on  Extra Crunch Live, where we’ll be interviewing him as well as taking questions from the audience.
 Levchin could not be more central to the story of Silicon Valley’s rise, and the rise of fintech, in the last twenty years. As one of the co-founders of  PayPal,   he’s been at the center of how we use the internet to send and spend money from its earliest days. And as the CEO of  Affirm, one of the hottest fintech companies around today, you can safely say he’s still in the game and winning.
 But wait! There’s more! All that’s just part of Max’s fintech credentials. He’s also currently the chairman of health tech startup Glow, and his past roles have included chairman of Yelp and member of the board of Yahoo, and much more.
 We are living in truly crazy times today, with the global health pandemic impacting every aspect of our lives, no less our tech lives. Max’s track record, and his own story as an immigrant building huge businesses in America, make him a very compelling person to weigh in on all of that. So please join us to watch, and participate in the conversation.
 Extra Crunch Live is open exclusively to  Extra Crunch subscribers. If you’re not already an Extra Crunch member, you can  join here. We have  the whole schedule of Extra Crunch Live talks as well.
 I’ll be in the interviewer’s chair, and I plan to grill Max on all things fintech and foundery — where financial tech startups are going, how they are faring now, what founders need to be thinking about, and how to avoid big mistakes. I’m also really looking forward to what you, the audience, want to ask Max, too.
 See you later for all the fun, Thursday August 6 at 4pm EDT / 1pm PDT / 8pm GMT. The links are below the fold.

然而，推特表示，这些标签是“保护这种言论的更大努力的一部分，因为我们认为政治影响力应该是赢得的，而不是收买的。”
当谈到给政府官员贴标签时，该公司表示，它将重点放在代表“国家在海外的官方声音”的人身上，包括“外交部长、机构实体、大使、官方发言人和主要外交领导人”。首先是联合国安理会五个常任理事国：中国、法国、俄罗斯、英国和美国，并计划在未来增加其他国家。
推特表示，这些标签将不适用于“国家元首的个人账户”，因为“这些账户享有广泛的知名度、媒体关注和公众意识。”例如：唐纳德·特朗普总统的推特账户还没有贴上标签，但国务卿迈克·庞皮欧的账户已经贴上了标签。
至于国家附属媒体，推特表示，尽管有政府资助，但仍保持编辑独立性的媒体组织，如BBC和NPR，将不会被贴上标签。
取而代之的是，这个标签将被保留给“国家通过财政资源、直接或间接的政治压力和/或对生产和分销的控制来控制编辑内容的媒体”-例如，俄罗斯支持的RT。为了找出这些渠道，该公司表示，它正在咨询外部专家，包括其数字和人权咨询小组(Twitter信托与安全委员会的一部分)的成员。
推特还表示，官方媒体将不再通过该服务的主页时间表、通知和搜索获得宣传。(这一限制不适用于政府官员。)。在去年中国官方通讯社购买推广推文，将香港的亲民主抗议活动描绘成暴力事件后，该服务已经阻止了这些团体购买广告。
note_en:
“Twitter provides an unmatched way to connect with, and directly speak to public officials and representatives,” the company wrote in  the blog post announcing these changes. “This direct line of communication with leaders and officials has helped to democratize political discourse and increase transparency and accountability.”
 However, Twitter suggested that these labels are part of a larger effort “to protect that discourse because we believe political reach should be earned not bought.”
  When it comes to labeling government officials, the company said it’s focusing on those who represent “the official voice of the state abroad,” including “foreign ministers, institutional entities, ambassadors, official spokespeople, and key diplomatic leaders.” It’s starting with the five permanent members of the United Nations Security Council: China, France, Russia, the United Kingdom and the United States, with plans to add other countries in the future.
 Twitter said these labels will not apply to “the personal accounts of heads of state,” because “these accounts enjoy widespread name recognition, media attention, and public awareness.” For example: President  Donald Trump’s Twitter account has not been labeled, but Secretary of State  Mike Pompeo’s account has.
  As for state-affiliated media, Twitter said that media organizations that maintain editorial independence despite government financing, such as the BBC and NPR, will not labeled.
 Instead, the label will be reserved for “outlets where the state exercises control over editorial content through financial resources, direct or indirect political pressures, and/or control over production and distribution” — for example,  Russia-backed RT. To identify these outlets, the company says it’s consulting outside experts, including members of its Digital and Human Rights Advisory group (part of Twitter’s Trust & Safety Council).
 Twitter also said state-affiliated media will no longer receive promotion via the service’s home timeline, notifications and search. (This limitation does not apply to government officials.) The service had already  blocked these groups from purchasing advertising after an incident last year in which  China’s state news agency bought promoted tweets to portray pro-democracy protests in Hong Kong as violent.
title: 推特为政府官员和国家控制的媒体添加标签
title_en: Twitter adds labels for government officials and state-controlled media

note:
谷歌已经停止生产去年10月发布的旗舰手机Pixel4和Pixel4XL。这两款设备在美国的谷歌商店都脱销了，尽管一些变种目前在其他地区仍然可以买到。
一位谷歌发言人向The Verge证实：“谷歌商店已经售出了Pixel4[和]4XL的库存，并完成了销售。”“对于那些仍然有兴趣购买Pixel4[和]4XL的人来说，有些合作伙伴可以提供该产品，而供货可以持续。就像所有Pixel设备一样，Pixel 4将在至少三年内继续获得软件和安全更新，从该设备首次在美国的谷歌商店上市之日起算。“。
谷歌这么快就停产Pixel手机是不寻常的。Pixels 2和Pixels 3各自的销售时间都在18个月左右，谷歌在推出它们的继任者大约6个月后停止了销售。此举意味着，从技术上讲，谷歌不再有旗舰手机可供销售。
不过，这只是个技术细节-谷歌本周早些时候推出了新的Pixel4A，将于8月20日发布，并证实Pixel5和配备5G的4A将于今年秋天晚些时候发布。虽然4A是一款经济型手机，但在电池续航时间更长，边框设计更少，主摄像头同样好的情况下，你可能会更喜欢它，而不是普通的Pixel 4。这是迪特尔在我们的评论中得出的结论，他说：“Pixel 4A比其他Pixel手机更有说服力。”
note_en:
Google has discontinued  the Pixel 4 and Pixel 4 XL, its flagship phones that were released in October of last year. Both devices are out of stock in Google’s store in the US, though some variants are still available in other regions for the time being.
 “Google Store has sold through its inventory and completed sales of Pixel 4 [and] 4 XL,” a Google spokesperson confirms to  The Verge. “For people who are still interested in buying Pixel 4 [and] 4 XL, the product is available from some partners while supplies last. Just like all Pixel devices, Pixel 4 will continue to get software and security updates for at least three years from when the device first became available on the Google Store in the US.”
 It’s unusual for Google to discontinue a Pixel phone so quickly. The Pixels  2 and  3 were on sale for around 18 months each, with Google stopping sales roughly six months after the introduction of their successors. The move means Google technically no longer has a flagship phone for sale.
 That is a technicality, though — Google introduced the new Pixel 4A earlier this week for an August 20th release, and also confirmed that  the Pixel 5 and a 5G-equipped 4A would be coming later this fall. And while the 4A is a budget device, between its better battery life, less bezel-y design, and equally good main camera you might actually prefer it to the regular Pixel 4. That’s the conclusion Dieter came to in  our review, saying “the Pixel 4A makes a much stronger case for itself than other Pixel phones.”
title: 谷歌已经停止生产Pixel4和4XL
title_en: Google Has Already Discontinued the Pixel 4 and 4 XL

note:
Twitter表示，它将不再通过将国有媒体机构的推文排除在其推荐系统之外，来放大这些推文。
此举降低了受影响的账户出现在搜索结果、通知和用户时间线上的可能性。
该公司还将标记与政府有关联的媒体的账户，以及来自中国、法国、俄罗斯、英国和美国的主要政府官员的账户。
Twitter表示，它正在采取行动，为人们提供更多关于他们在社交网络上看到的内容的背景信息。
其他社交平台，包括视频分享巨头YouTube，已经给国家支持的媒体组织的频道贴上了标签。
推特将给媒体组织贴上标签，在这些媒体组织中，国家通过财政资源、政治压力或对制作和发行的控制来控制编辑内容。
然而，拥有编辑自主权的公共资助的新闻机构将不会被贴上标签，包括BBC和美国网络NPR。
推特称，将给主要政府官员贴上标签，包括中国、法国、俄罗斯、英国和美国这五个联合国安理会常任理事国的外长、机构、大使和发言人。
推特表示，我们的重点是在海外代表民族国家声音的高级官员和实体。
例如，特朗普总统广受欢迎的推特账号@realDonaldTrump将不会被贴上标签，而他的官方账号@Potus将会被识别。
推特在解释这一决定时表示，个人账户享有广泛的知名度、媒体关注和公众知名度。
推特表示，随着时间的推移，它将扩大标签账户的名单，并包括更多的国家。
note_en:
Twitter says it will no longer "amplify" tweets by state-controlled media organisations, by excluding them from its recommendation systems.
 The move makes affected accounts less likely to appear in search results, notifications and on a user's timeline.
 The company will also label the accounts of government-linked media, as well as "key government officials" from China, France, Russia, the UK and US.
  Twitter said it was acting to provide people with more context about what they see on the social network.
  Other social platforms, including video-sharing giant YouTube, already label the channels of state-backed media organisations.
  Twitter will label media organisations "where the state exercises control over editorial content through financial resources, political pressure or control over production and distribution".
  However, publicly funded news organisations with editorial independence will not be labelled, including the BBC and the US network NPR.
  Twitter says it will label key government officials, including foreign ministers, institutions, ambassadors and spokesmen and women from the five permanent members of the UN Security Council: China, France, Russia, the UK and US.
 "Our focus is on senior officials and entities that are the voice of the nation state abroad," Twitter said.
  For example, President Trump's popular Twitter account @realDonaldTrump will not be labelled, while his official account @Potus will be identified.
 Explaining the decision, Twitter said personal accounts "enjoy widespread name recognition, media attention, and public awareness".
 Twitter said it would expand the list of labelled accounts and include additional countries over time.
title: 推特表示，它将开始为来自中国、法国、俄罗斯、英国和美国的与政府有关联的媒体账户以及“主要政府官员”贴上标签
title_en: Twitter says it will start labeling government-linked media accounts, as well as “key government officials”, from China, France, Russia, the UK, and US

note:
跳到主要内容我已经对新的27英寸iMac进行了大约两天的评论-足够长的时间开始戳它，给你一些它是什么样子的印象。IMac的大部分改进都达到了你在2020年的电脑中预期的规格：第10代英特尔处理器、固态硬盘标准，等等。我的设备玻璃上也有苹果500美元的纳米纹理处理，苹果说这比传统的哑光显示屏有了很大的改进。(如果是500美元，最好是这样。)。
但有一个技术指标的提升对苹果来说是非常不寻常的，即使在这个大流行的年份也是如此：网络摄像头的质量终于得到了改善。如果你经常开视频会议，新的1080p网络摄像头很可能是最能改善你的日常生活的东西。我不想告诉你这一点，但是当你的相机更清晰一点的时候，在你的同事看来，你确实看起来更专业了。
当然，我不认为仅仅为了摄像头就升级到新的iMac是值得的，但我很高兴苹果把它做得更好了。我也不知道我会不会说这是我用过的最好的，但它不再像苹果的大多数其他网络摄像头那样隐约令人尴尬。
我们就把货给你吧。这是不同之处，取自QuickTime录像机的静止帧。我工作时恰好有一台2017年的iMac，所以这是一个直接的对比：
网络摄像头之所以更好，不仅仅是因为它有更多的像素，还因为苹果最终将一些现代图像处理应用到视频流中。IMac有一个T2芯片，用于控制Mac中的许多组件。苹果公司正在用它来处理这个网络摄像头图像的某些元素。它能够进行色调映射、曝光控制和面部检测。
脸部检测是为了优先考虑，让你的脸保持良好的光照和准确的肤色。我可以在画面中移动我的脸，看到它实时调整曝光，确保我的脸永远不会太黑或太大。这感觉非常像iPhone对脸部的处理。(如果你想知道，苹果公司说它没有做任何面部平滑处理。)。
它运行得非常好，幸运的是，它不仅仅适用于苹果自己的应用程序。由于这些修复都是通过T2芯片实现的，因此性能的提升只是任何视频会议应用程序都能获得的网络摄像头视频流。
有一件事让人感觉2020年的iMac一点也不现代，那就是登录。除非你有一块Apple Watch，并用它解锁你的电脑，否则唯一的进入方式就是像动物一样输入你的密码。苹果的T2芯片控制Mac笔记本电脑上的Touch ID指纹登录，但苹果选择不在这款iMac的键盘或面部ID阵列上添加指纹传感器。
这很烦人，但这也是苹果决定不改变这款iMac设计的一个功能。它有和以前一样的萨诺式下巴，同样的屏幕，同样的端口。
不过，在这些领域也有一些升级。屏幕是一样的，但我一直提到的T2芯片意味着你可以打开True Tone来匹配你房间的色彩平衡。
另一个重大升级是纳米纹理选项。很棒，但是我有些保留意见。但首先，这里的一张照片显示，它发挥了作用，减少了眩光：
不幸的是，我的前两大保留意见是那些不能通过两天的测试就能解决的事情。其一是价格：500美元的价格是超级昂贵的升级，只有你对眩光的容忍度才能告诉你它是否物有所值。另一件事可能会帮助你决定它是否值得，那就是漆面是否耐用。这是我的第二个预订：我只是不知道。
为了解释原因，我需要解释一下这种纳米质地抛光是什么。苹果不只是在玻璃上涂上一层哑光涂层，而是在玻璃上进行纳米级的蚀刻。这一过程使纳米纹理处理比传统的哑光屏幕更有优势，因为图像看起来不会模糊化。在无光屏幕上，像素发出的光会散开。苹果公司声称，苹果的抛光主要是散射从外部照射的光线，而不会散射来自像素的光线。
对于这个问题，这是一个非常花哨、非常昂贵的解决方案。很有苹果味。也很苹果：它附带的说明书规定你只能清洁附带的超细纤维布料，否则可能会损坏抛光效果。哎呀。
在5,000美元的Pro Display XDR上使用精致的屏幕是一回事，专业成年人在专业设置中使用这些屏幕是一回事。在家庭娱乐室的iMac上有一个精致的屏幕，满身泥土的孩子们会用爪子摸它，因为他们正确地认为所有的屏幕都应该是触摸屏，这完全是另一回事。
我问苹果公司关于漆面耐用性的问题。有人告诉我，他们不想给任何人留下易碎的印象，但是，是的，随着时间的推移，使用太过研磨的东西可能会破坏抛光效果。与其他屏幕不同的是，在纳米表面上真的没有涂层；它只是蚀刻的裸露玻璃。
我怀疑除了苹果之外，没有任何人有足够多的用户数据来说明XDR的纹理表现如何，这些数据可以告诉你，在家用iMac上付费是否是一件负责任的事情。
所以，再说一次，我不能告诉你这是不是一个好的选择。但我可以告诉你，它很管用。它完全消除了眩光，以至于我第一次能够把电脑放在起居室里，身后有一扇窗户。它对屏幕上图像或文本的清晰度也没有太大影响-但如果你真的眯着眼睛仔细看，你会看到一点模糊。
这种东西几乎不可能拍下来。(我试过了。)。你只需要相信我，它几乎是不可察觉的，从一个角度来看，一点也不差。
除了这两件大事之外，我没有太多东西可以告诉你，这是一堆基准测试无法涵盖的。它看起来和操作起来都像一台速度非常快的iMac。一个不只是渲染时间的具体例子：使用标准化的SSD早就应该了，因为我的旧iMac肯定会有奇怪的停顿，因为它正在旋转Fusion Drive中的硬盘。
在购买这款iMac的决定过程中还有一个变数：在未来两年的某个时候，它将被另一款使用苹果自己的ARM处理器而不是英特尔处理器的机型取代。据推测，这款iMac最终会得到更新，消除了按喇叭下巴和无法通过生物识别登录的问题。但今天还不能说ARM Mac是否物有所值--正如人们猜测的那样，你是否应该等待它们。
苹果已经给出了所有的迹象，表明它将在很长一段时间内完全支持基于英特尔的Mac，我非常希望你所需要的程序和应用程序也将在很长一段时间内继续兼容基于英特尔的Mac。
我的意思是：如果你需要一台27英寸的iMac，这是一台不错的iMac，你应该买它。很抱歉，这个过程将包括45分钟的复选和取消复选纳米纹理选项，然后您再做决定。

note_en:
Skip to main content                                                                                                                I’ve had a review unit of the new 27-inch iMac for about two days now — long enough to start poking at it and give you some impressions of what it’s like. Most of the improvements on the iMac bring it up to the specs you’d expect in a 2020 computer: 10th Gen Intel processors, SSDs standard, and so on. My unit also has Apple’s $500 nano texture finish on the glass, which Apple says is a big improvement over traditional matte displays. (For $500, it had better be.)
 But there’s one spec bump that is wildly out of character for Apple, even in this pandemic year: the quality of the webcam has finally been improved. If you’re videoconferencing a lot, the new 1080p webcam is likely going to be the thing that improves your day-to-day the most. I hate to tell you this, but you really do look more professional to your colleagues when your camera is just a little sharper.
 I don’t think it’s worth upgrading to a new iMac just for the webcam, of course, but I am glad that Apple has made it better. I also don’t know that I’d say it’s the best I’ve used, but it’s no longer vaguely embarrassing like most of Apple’s other webcams.
 Let’s just give you the goods. Here’s the difference, taken from a still frame out of the QuickTime recorder. I happen to have a 2017 iMac for work, so it’s a direct comparison:
  The webcam isn’t just better because it has more pixels; it’s also better because Apple is finally applying some modern image processing to the video stream. The iMac has a T2 chip, which is used to control lots of the components in the Mac. Apple is using it to process certain elements of this webcam’s image. It is able to do tone mapping, exposure control, and face detection.
   The face detection is for prioritizing keeping your face well-lit with accurate skin tones. I can move my face around in the frame and see it adjusting the exposure in real time, ensuring that my face is never too dark or blown-out. It feels very much like what the iPhone does with faces. (Apple says it’s not doing any face smoothing, if you’re wondering.)
 It works really well, and luckily, it doesn’t just work in Apple’s own apps. Since these fixes are coming via the T2 chip, the improved performance is just the webcam video stream that any videoconferencing app will get.
  One thing that doesn’t feel modern at all with the 2020 iMac is logging in. Unless you have an Apple Watch and use it to unlock your computer, the only way to get in is to type out your password like an animal. Apple’s T2 chip controls Touch ID fingerprint login on Mac laptops, but Apple opted not to add a fingerprint sensor to the keyboard or a Face ID array in this iMac.
 It’s annoying, but it’s also a function of Apple’s decision to not change anything about the design of this iMac. It has the same Thano-esque chin, the same screen, and the same ports as before.
 There are some upgrades in those areas, though. The screen is identical, but that T2 chip I keep mentioning means that you can turn on True Tone to match it to the color balance in your room.
 The other big upgrade is that nano texture option. It is great, but I have some reservations. But first, here’s a photo showing that it does its job, reducing glare:
  My first two big reservations are, unfortunately, the sorts of things that can’t be resolved with just two days of testing. One is the price: at $500, it’s a super expensive upgrade, and only your tolerance for glare can tell you if it’s worth the price. Another thing that might help you decide if it’s worth is whether the finish is durable. That’s my second reservation: I just don’t know.
 To explain why, I need to explain what this nano texture finish even is. Instead of just putting a matte coating on top of the glass, Apple is literally etching the glass at a nanometer scale. That process gives the nano texture finish a leg up on traditional matte screens in that images won’t look fuzzified. On matte screens, the light from the pixels gets scattered  out. Apple’s finish, the company claims, mainly diffuses the light that hits from the outside and doesn’t scatter the light from the pixels as much.
 It’s a very fancy, very expensive solution to the problem. It’s very Apple. Also very Apple: the instructions that come with it that specify that you  should only clean it the included microfiber cloth and that doing otherwise could damage the finish. Yikes.
 A delicate screen on a $5,000 Pro Display XDR used in professional settings by professional adults who know what they have is one thing. A delicate screen on the iMac in the family room where dirt-covered children will paw at it because they rightly assume all screens should be touchscreens is something else entirely.
  I asked Apple about the durability of the finish. I was told that they don’t want to give anybody the impression that it’s fragile, but that, yes: over time, using something too abrasive could mess up that finish. Unlike other screens, there’s really no coating on top of the nano finish; it’s just etched, bare glass.
 I doubt that anybody but Apple has a critical mass of user data on how the texture has fared on the XDR that could inform you whether it’s a responsible thing to pay for on a family iMac.
 So, again, I can’t tell you if it’s a good option. But I can tell you that it works great. It fully eliminates glare to the point where, for the first time, I was able to position my computer with a window behind me in my living room. It also doesn’t affect the sharpness of images or text on the screen much at all — but if you truly squint up close, you can see a little fuzziness.
 This sort of thing is nearly impossible to photograph. (I’ve tried.) You’ll just have to trust me that it’s nigh imperceptible straight on and not bad at all at an angle.
  Beyond those two big things, I don’t have a lot to tell you that can’t be covered by a bunch of benchmarks. It looks and operates like a very fast iMac. One tangible example that isn’t just about render times: going to a standardized SSD is long overdue because there are definitely moments when my old iMac has odd pauses because it’s spinning up the hard disk in the Fusion Drive.
 There’s one more variable in the decision process of buying this iMac: at some point in the next two years, it will be replaced by another model that uses Apple’s own ARM processors instead of Intel’s. Presumably, that iMac will finally get a refresh that does away with the honking chin and inability to log in biometrically. But whether ARM Macs are a good buy is impossible to say today — as is guessing if you should wait for them.
 Apple has given every indication that it will fully support Intel-based Macs for a long time, and I have every hope that the programs and apps you need will continue to be compatible with Intel-based Macs for a long time as well.
 What I’m saying is: if you need a 27-inch iMac, this is a good iMac, and you should buy it. I’m sorry that the process is going to include 45 minutes of checking and unchecking the nano texture option while you make up your mind.

note:
当我们试图理解Google 2020 Pixel阵容的意义时，9to5 Google审阅了一份Android内部文档，让我们第一次看到了明年会发生什么。根据这份文件，谷歌正在为Pixel 5a、可折叠Pixel以及至少另外两款第一方设备开发Android版本。
这份文件列出了自Pixel2以来所有公开发布的Pixels的谷歌移动操作系统的构建，包括Pixel4a，Pixel4a 5G，Pixel5，迄今尚未宣布的Google Pixel5a(似乎代号为Barbette)，以及其他三款代号为“Raven”、“Oriole”和“Passport”的正在开发的设备。(该文件还指出，这些代号可能与AOSP中使用的代号不同，安卓粉丝知道AOSP通常是以鱼类命名的。)。
“乌鸦”和“金莺”可能是“Pixel6”型号(文件没有具体说明)，但第三种装置“Passport”被明确称为“可折叠”。这三款手机都被贴上了“2021年第四季度”的标签，这可能是Pixel可折叠智能手机正在向零售产品迈进的早期迹象。
2019年，CNET报道称谷歌正在“研发可折叠的Pixel”，但当时的Pixel Head马里奥·奎罗斯(Mario Queiroz)只是明确证实，该公司正在“制作这项技术的原型”，并在当时补充说，谷歌已经这样做了“很长一段时间”。当时他说，他认为“还没有明确的用例”，并补充说，“现在，你不需要有一个可折叠的。”这是一种“可有可无”的感觉。
该文件还指出，Pixel4a(5G)和Pixel5定于“10月”发布，与谷歌宣布的“秋季”一致，鉴于Pixel4a的延迟，这一点值得注意。除此之外，还明确提到了Pixel5a的名字和相关的发射时间框架“2021年第二季度”。该设备--最近首次出现在AOSP上--也被进一步描述为“年中低端设备”。
虽然这份内部文档确实指向了Pixel未来的发布，并反映了目前正在进行的工作，但它的主要目的是为了Android开发。因此，它可能无法反映谷歌硬件团队的具体计划。例如，本文档中出现了一个代号为“Muskie”(Axed Pixel2XL原型)的设备，明确表示并非列表中的所有设备都能投入生产。
你正在阅读朝九晚五的谷歌新闻，这些专家日复一日地发布关于谷歌及其周边生态系统的新闻。请务必查看我们的主页以获取所有最新新闻，并关注Twitter、Facebook和LinkedIn上的朝九晚五谷歌，随时了解最新消息。不知道从何说起？查看我们的独家故事、评论、操作指南，并订阅我们的YouTube频道
note_en:
As we  try to make sense of Google’s 2020 Pixel lineup, an internal Android document reviewed by  9to5Google gives us our first glimpse of what to expect next year. According to the document, Google is developing Android builds for Pixel 5a, a foldable Pixel, and at least two other first-party devices.
  The document lists builds of Google’s mobile operating system for all publicly-launched Pixels since Pixel 2, including Pixel 4a, Pixel 4a 5G, Pixel 5, the as-yet unannounced Google Pixel 5a (seemingly codenamed  barbette), and three other in-development devices with codenames “ raven,” “ oriole,” and “ passport.” (The document also notes that these codenames may differ from those used in AOSP, which Android fans know are generally named after fish.)
 It’s possible that “ raven” and “ oriole” could be “Pixel 6” models (the document doesn’t specify), but the third device, “ passport” is explicitly referred to as being a “foldable.” All three are labeled as “Q4 2021,” which could be an early indication that a Pixel foldable smartphone is advancing toward becoming a retail product.
 In 2019,   CNET reported that Google was “working on a foldable Pixel,” but Pixel head at the time Mario Queiroz only explicitly confirmed that the company was “prototyping the technology,” adding at the time that Google had been doing that “for a long time”. At the time he said he didn’t think “there’s a clear use case yet,” adding, “Right now, you don’t need to have a foldable. It’s kind of a ‘nice-to-have.'”
 The document also notes that Pixel 4a (5G) and Pixel 5 are slated for “October,” in line with Google’s  announcement of “fall” and notable given the Pixel 4a  delay. Beyond that, there’s explicit mention by name of the Pixel 5a and an associated launch timeframe of “Q2 2021.” That device — which recently  appeared in AOSP for the first time — is also further described as a “lower end mid year device.”
 While this internal document does point toward future Pixel launches and reflect those currently in the works, its primary purpose is for Android development. As a result, it may not mirror the concrete plans of Google’s hardware team. For example, one device, codenamed “muskie” (an axed  Pixel 2 XL prototype) shows up in this document, making clear that not all devices on the list make it to production.
  You’re reading 9to5Google — experts who break news about Google and its surrounding ecosystem, day after day. Be sure to check out  our homepage for all the latest news, and follow 9to5Google on  Twitter,  Facebook, and  LinkedIn to stay in the loop. Don’t know where to start? Check out our  exclusive stories,  reviews,  how-tos, and  subscribe to our YouTube channel
title: Android内部开发文件显示，谷歌正在为Pixel5a、Follible Pixel和至少另外两款第一方设备进行构建
title_en: Internal Android development document suggests Google is working on builds for a Pixel 5a, foldable Pixel, and at least two other first-party devices

note:
据路透社报道，周三，美国司法部要求一名联邦法官阻止加州关键的网络中立法。
2017年，特朗普联邦通信委员会(Trump Federal Communications Commission)投票废除了奥巴马时代的互联网法规，该法规禁止互联网服务提供商限制或阻止流量，并建立付费快车道。2018年8月，加利福尼亚州通过了自己的法律，在州一级维护了这些网络中立原则；现在，美国政府正在寻求初步禁令，在州政府能够执行之前阻止这项法律。
该法律通过后不久，司法部对加利福尼亚州提起诉讼，但由于对最初FCC命令的法律挑战得到裁决，案件被搁置。根据这一最新要求，随着案件的进行，司法部正寻求暂停加州法律的实施。
加州的法律去年赢得了第一次挑战。在FCC于2017年推翻其网络中立规则后，该机构的决定在法庭上受到了包括Mozilla在内的组织联盟的挑战。请愿人辩称，联邦通信委员会的决定是非法的，基于对互联网服务市场分析不力，会损害公共安全。加利福尼亚州同意在法院对此案做出裁决之前不执行自己的法律，因为FCC在翻案中加入了先发制人的语言，这些语言将先发制人，阻止各州推出自己的网络中立规则。
2019年10月，联邦通信委员会的逆转大多得到支持，但哥伦比亚特区上诉法院裁定，联邦通信委员会无权禁止各州通过自己的网络中立法规。据路透社(Reuters)报道，司法部认为，FCC的裁决先发制人，抢占了加州等州的法律。
加州总检察长办公室没有立即回复The Verge的置评请求，但告诉路透社，该办公室正在审查司法部的文件。
司法部提交的文件预计至少要到10月中旬才会做出决定。
note_en:
On Wednesday, the US Justice Department asked a federal judge to block California’s pivotal net neutrality law,  according to  Reuters.
 In 2017, the Trump Federal Communications Commission voted to repeal the Obama-era internet regulations that barred internet service providers from throttling or blocking traffic and instituting paid fast lanes. In August 2018, California passed its own law  upholding those net neutrality principles at the state level; now, the US government is seeking a preliminary injunction to block the law before the state is able to enforce it.
 The Department of Justice filed suit against California  soon after the law was passed, but the case was put on hold as legal challenges to the initial FCC order were adjudicated. With this latest request, the Justice Department is seeking to suspend implementation of the California law as the case proceeds.
  The California law won its first challenge last year. After the FCC reversed its net neutrality rules in 2017, the agency’s decision was challenged in court by a coalition of organizations including Mozilla. The petitioners argued that the FCC’s decision was unlawful, founded on poor analysis of the internet service market, and would harm public safety. California agreed not to enforce its own law before the courts ruled on this case because the FCC had included language in its reversal that would preempt states from rolling out their own net neutrality rules.
 In October 2019, the FCC’s reversal was  mostly upheld, but the District of Columbia Court of Appeals ruled that the FCC did not have the legal authority to prohibit states from passing their own net neutrality regulations. According to  Reuters, the Justice Department believes that the FCC’s ruling preempts state laws like California’s.
 The California attorney general’s office did not immediately respond to a request for comment from  The Verge but told  Reuters that the office was reviewing the Justice Department’s filing.
 A decision on the Justice Department’s filing isn’t expected until at least mid-October.
title: 联邦政府采取行动阻止加州的网络中立法
title_en: Feds move to block California’s net neutrality law

note:
微软今天结束了针对iOS设备的XCloud游戏流媒体测试。在过去的几个月里，这家软件巨头一直在以非常有限的方式在iOS上测试XCloud，但它明确表示，这项服务本周早些时候才会在Android上推出。微软已经通知XCloud测试人员，预览版将在9月11日结束，但只有Android预览版将持续到下个月。
微软一位发言人在给The Verge的一份声明中表示：“我们在iOS上的XCloud预览版TestFlight阶段已经结束，我们正专注于从9月15日开始向Android客户提供云游戏，作为Xbox Game Pass Ultimate的一部分。”我们的雄心是通过在所有设备上都可以使用的Xbox Game Pass来扩展云游戏。“。
微软在iOS上推出云流媒体的雄心遇到了一些障碍。该公司不得不将其XCloud iOS测试应用程序限制为仅限Halo：the Master General Collection和10000名测试员，因为“为了遵守App Store的政策”。
微软没有透露哪些苹果政策阻止XCloud在iOS上推出，但谷歌的Stadia游戏流媒体服务也不能在iPhone或iPad上使用。谷歌只提供场馆管理应用程序，但不涉及游戏流媒体。其他游戏流媒体应用程序也在App Store上面临问题。Valve的Steam Link应用程序花了近一年的时间才获得苹果的批准，尽管它主要是从你的家用PC上播放游戏。
XCloud在iOS上的未来仍然不明朗，可能不在微软手中。这些问题似乎至少在一定程度上与苹果通过其App Store进行应用内购买的规定有关。苹果对“远程桌面客户端”也有严格的限制，这意味着应用程序只能连接到用户拥有的主机设备或用户拥有的游戏机。主机设备和客户端也必须连接到本地网络。虽然微软可能会绕过应用内购买App Store的策略，但远程桌面客户端规则可能是更大的障碍。
微软正在通过谷歌Play Store版本的Xbox Game Pass避免应用内购买，该版本将包括游戏流媒体(XCloud)。取而代之的是，微软与三星合作，提供了一款特殊版本的Xbox Game Pass应用程序，允许Xbox玩家兑换代币，并在应用程序内进行购买，比如在Xbox Store购买皮肤或DLC商品。
微软目前正计划在9月15日推出游戏流媒体(XCloud)，作为Xbox Game Pass旗舰版的一部分。这项服务在发布时将专属于Android平板电脑和手机，但微软也在开发一款PC应用程序，将Xbox游戏流式传输到Windows10机器上。
note_en:
Microsoft has ended its xCloud game streaming test for iOS devices today. The software giant had been testing xCloud on iOS in a  very limited way over the past few months, but made it clear the service would  only be launching on Android earlier this week. Microsoft had informed xCloud testers that the preview would end on September 11th, but only the Android preview will continue until next month.
 “Our Project xCloud preview TestFlight period has ended on iOS and we are focused on delivering cloud gaming as part of Xbox Game Pass Ultimate to Android customers beginning September 15,” says a Microsoft spokesperson in a statement to  The Verge. “It’s our ambition to scale cloud gaming through Xbox Game Pass available on all devices.”
 Microsoft’s ambitions to launch cloud streaming on iOS have hit some obstacles. The company had to limit its xCloud iOS test app to just  Halo: The Master Chief Collection and 10,000 testers due “to comply with App Store policies.”
   Microsoft hasn’t revealed which Apple policies are preventing xCloud from launching on iOS, but Google’s Stadia game streaming service is also not available on iPhones or iPads. Google merely supplies a Stadia management app, but there’s no game streaming involved. Other game streaming apps have also faced issues on the App Store.  Valve’s Steam Link app took nearly a year for Apple to approve even though it primarily streams games from your home PC.
 The future of xCloud on iOS remains unclear and potentially out of Microsoft’s hands. The issues appear to be related, in part at least, to Apple’s rules on in-app purchases through its App Store. Apple also  has strict limits on “remote desktop clients” that mean apps are only allowed to connect to a user-owned host device or game console owned by the user. Both the host device and client must be connected on a local network, too. While Microsoft could potentially work around the in-app purchase App Store policies, the remote desktop client rules are likely the bigger hurdle.
 Microsoft is avoiding in-app purchases with the Google Play Store version of Xbox Game Pass that will include game streaming (xCloud). Instead, Microsoft has partnered with Samsung to offer a  special version of the Xbox Game Pass app that will allow Xbox players to redeem tokens and make in-app purchases like buying skins or DLC items in the Xbox Store.
 Microsoft is now planning to launch game streaming (xCloud) as part of Xbox Game Pass Ultimate on September 15th. The service will be exclusive to Android tablets and phones at launch, but Microsoft is also  working on a PC app to stream Xbox games to Windows 10 machines.
title: 微软提前削减XCloud iOS测试
title_en: Microsoft cuts xCloud iOS testing early

note:
当“无题鹅游戏”终于在今年晚些时候正式发布时(继去年数字版大获成功之后)，它还将首次亮相一个新的生态包装系列。发行商iam8bit表示，它希望引领整个行业走向未来。
Iam8bit&39；所谓的可爱版游戏的发布将是PS4的第一款游戏，它将摒弃标准的塑料蓝光DVD盒，转而采用100%消费后的可回收材料制成的纸板盒，这种纸盒的库存很重，没有有害的墨水。包装还使用了一种名为生物烯烃的可生物降解塑料包装纸，这种塑料在一到三年后就会分解成生物量，而不是使用生物烯烃。包装使用的是耐用的20磅原料，没有有害的墨水。包装还使用了一种名为生物烯烃的可生物降解的塑料包装纸，这种包装纸在一到三年后就会分解成生物量，而不是有害的墨水。
生态友好的决定也延伸到游戏盒里的东西。包裹中包含的小册子和折叠式海报从森林管理委员会认证的来源获得纸张，确保木材只来自管理最好、对环境最负责任的森林，正如iam8bit所说。所附的No Goose贴纸是由甘蔗废料制成的，该公司向我们保证，甘蔗废料是完全可生物降解的，但并不像听起来那么美味。
根据iam8bit的说法，创造一个符合收藏意识的游戏玩家标准的纸盒是一个挑战，需要一年的迭代。Iam8bit的共同所有者乔恩·吉布森(Jon Gibson)告诉Ars，要让它被消费者接受，仅仅是对我们的星球采取更友好的方式是不够的。包装必须与每个人习惯的包装放在一起-它不能看起来便宜或脆弱。它必须给人一种尊贵、优雅的感觉，最重要的是，要实实在在。
title: 无名的鹅游戏抛弃了塑料，推出了环保的游戏盒
title_en: Untitled Goose Game ditches plastic for its eco-friendly game cases

note:
今天，我们来看看赛德工作室的奥德赛X86J4105--一款制造商/建造商量身定做的赛扬驱动的迷你PC。这款小小的设备看起来就像是Chromebox和Raspberry Pi的甜蜜爱情--它是一块赛扬供电的一体式系统级芯片(SoC)板，免费出售，配有与Raspberry Pi兼容的GPIO接口和Arduino协处理器，可用于更多基于硬件的制造商项目。
我要承认：我从来没有真正喜欢过树莓派。异端，我知道！但是，尽管这些备受喜爱的小盒子真的很便宜，但对于我感兴趣的项目来说，它们似乎从来都不够强大。有时，我会与其他价格稍高、功能更强大的ARM迷你电脑调情，比如OdroidXU4或更新的OdroidN2，但与廉价的x86电脑相比，它们仍然感觉相当有限。奥德赛似乎是为解决这些性能问题而量身定做的。
(Ars Technica可能会通过会员计划从本帖子上的链接获得销售补偿。)。
按照台式机的标准，奥德赛的四核赛扬SoC可能算不上是一款强大的产品，但它足以运行完整的Windows10桌面体验。外加8G内存、64 GB eMMC存储器、一个SATA-III端口、两个1Gbps以太网插孔、双M.2插槽(一个B-Key和一个M-Key)、英特尔9560 Wi-Fi、英特尔UHD 600显卡和全尺寸HDMI端口，很难想象这款售价260美元的机顶盒能做什么。
如果您希望在非常低的水平上控制其他硬件，奥德赛还有一个与Raspberry PI兼容的40针GPIO接口和一个用于ATSAMD21 Arduino协处理器的28针接口。我们并不是为测试这些功能而设立的，但奥德赛制造商Seeed也是享有盛誉的格罗夫传感器系统的制造商-所以当它告诉我们奥德赛的连接器和协处理器与格罗夫兼容时，我们倾向于相信他们。
说到外形因素，奥德赛在它的重新计算机盒中最让我们想起了一款不同寻常的极客Chromebox。和Chromebox一样，Re_Computer外壳中的奥德赛也只比显示器背面的VESA安装板大--而且和Chromebox一样，它的背面也有VESA兼容的安装孔。不过，如果你想利用这个选项，你需要提供你自己的安装螺柱。
坦率地说，重新组装计算机的案例有点麻烦--提供的说明只由未标注的图表组成，而且图表并不都是准确的。尤其是，我们希望他们事先告诉我们，重新计算机的透明顶盖是磁性连接的！。盒子里的盖子是作为一个单独的部件列在库存里的，但它已经卡在箱子里了，现在还不清楚你能不能用铲子或其他锋利的工具很容易地撬开它。
一旦你真正组装好电脑机箱，它就会极具吸引力和功能性。您需要弄乱的任何部件都可以通过拆卸磁性连接的透明盖子来接近，并且外部端口都很容易到达并且不会被遮挡。我们也真的很喜欢表壳侧面被阳极化的欢快的皇家蓝色。
奥德赛强大的赛扬J4105处理器有大量的RAM、存储、网络和显卡功能作为后盾。无论您喜欢运行Windows还是Linux，它都可以处理CPU和IO受限的Raspberry PI系列难以处理的任务。如果您想构建高端DIY路由器，它具有双千兆以太网-而且J4105 CPU比我们自己的自制路由器中的1037U要强大得多。
如果您正在寻找建立一个科迪或类似的家庭影院PC系统，J4105和它的超高清600显卡已经超过了任务，至少高达1080p-4K是可以的，但一些视频将显示一些帧下降。在我们的测试中，Vimeo上的4K视频播放得无懈可击；YouTube上的4K视频是可以观看的，但也有明显的掉帧现象。
你甚至可以用“奥德赛”制作一个微型文件服务器。它提供一个可容纳高性能NVMe固态硬盘的M.2 PCIe 2.0 x4插槽，以及一个可连接到任何标准SATA驱动器的全尺寸SATA-III接口。将这种全速、可靠的连接添加到8GiB板载RAM中，您就有了足够的计算机来运行FreeNAS、XigmaNAS或即将推出的TrueNAS Core。
最后，你可以用它做一台非常有用的台式电脑。我们测试的版本预装了一个完全激活的Windows10企业版--它可以独立运行，也可以加入到域中。如果你更喜欢Linux台式机，你也可以在没有Windows10许可证的情况下购买奥德赛。
如果您使用过的任何用例都需要蜂窝连接，那么奥德赛也能满足您的需求--它的M.2 B键端口可以容纳LTE模块(不包括在内)，而且还有一个SIM卡插座。
奥德赛的8G内存和四核赛扬J4105，加上它的Sandisk 64 GB eMMC，足以让这个小盒子感觉像一台真正的电脑，即使在运行Windows的时候也是如此。我们的审查示例是在Windows10企业版中预安装和预激活的，它通常在15-30秒内完全冷启动到桌面(包括开机自检时间)。一旦到了那里，无论是安装应用程序还是使用Microsoft Edge浏览Web，它都感觉非常灵敏。
这个小系统附带了Windows10Build 1903，所以我们做的第一件事就是把它升级到2004。系统上安装的唯一非库存软件是Arduino软件包，因此我们这次没有拍摄WizTree和磁盘使用情况前后的图表-它是从Windows标准加载的，在升级前后它都非常适合板载64 GB eMMC。
我们在2016年推出的“黑帮”自制路由器(Homebrew Router)、今年的卡诺(Kano)个人电脑和沃尔玛(Walmart)该死的EVOO EV-C-116-5笔记本电脑上测试了奥德赛。奥德赛、OG Homebrew和卡诺PC都是基于赛扬的低功耗系统；AMDA4-9120驱动的EVOO应该属于同一类，但由于其大规模的工厂欠频，它不属于同一类。
早在2016年，当我们彻底测试具有挑战性的小数据包工作负载的路由性能时，我们发现Homebrew中的赛扬1037U足以在裸露的Ubuntu安装上以1Gbps的速度吊挂数据包。当我们测试pfSense时，更为用户友好且功能齐全的路由器发行版表现不佳。
尽管卡诺PC的N4000比Homebrew的1037U更新了四年，内核和线程都翻了一番，但它的速度并不是很快。相比之下，奥德赛的J4105是一个强大的引擎--它在多线程测试中比任何一个都要快得多，在单线程启动时也要快一点。
虽然我们还没有将奥德赛作为一款专用路由器进行直接测试，但它的基准测试让我们相信它会做得非常好。我们怀疑，即使在运行pfSense或eOPNense时，它也应该与运行香草Ubuntu的原版Homebrew安装得相当好。
我们对奥德赛X86J4105感到非常兴奋。它满足了几乎所有我们能想到的制造商或物联网项目的需要--它为桌面带来了比Raspberry Pi系列等基于ARM的设备，甚至比Pi&39；Odroid功能更强大的竞争对手强大得多的火力。
如果你正在寻找最便宜的解决方案，奥德赛可能不是你喜欢的，但如果你不介意多花一点钱，它完整的x86架构和令人惊叹的一系列连接所提供的灵活性是非常值得额外花钱的。标准x86体系结构还使您有可能脱离以硬件为中心的自定义系统映像的世界-相反，您只需从标准ISO直接将Windows、Linux或BSD安装到硬件上即可。
奥德赛也可以做一个很棒的孩子，电话亭，甚至是轻便的办公电脑。它很小，对于一个完全激活的Windows系统来说非常便宜，而且它的桌面体验非常好-比卡诺PC或典型的低端Chromebook要好得多。如果你选择64 GB的eMMC存储，它的速度和大小都足以让你在Windows 10上舒适地使用-或者，如果你想要更高的性能和更大的存储空间，你可以改用标准的M.2 PCIe或SATA SSD。
我们对奥德赛唯一的抱怨是，我们发现将附带的Wi-Fi天线连接到Intel9560芯片组是多么令人沮丧。这些图表没有正确标注芯片组在电路板上的实际位置，而且连接器的位置使得连接引线比在标准笔记本电脑上更令人沮丧。我们需要一台珠宝商的放大镜和大约10分钟的仔细、轻柔的小提琴和重试，最后我们才把两根导线正确地连接起来。
note_en:
Today we're going to take a look at Seeed Studio's Odyssey  X86J4105—a maker/builder-tailored, Celeron-powered mini-PC. The little device seems like what you'd get if a Chromebox and a Raspberry Pi made sweet, sweet love—it's a Celeron-powered all-in-one system-on-chip (SoC) board, sold without a case, with Raspberry Pi-compatible GPIO headers and an Arduino coprocessor for more hardware-based maker projects.
 I have a confession to make: I've never really loved the Raspberry Pi. Heresy, I know! But despite how seriously  cheap the much-loved little boxes are, they never seem quite powerful enough for the projects I'd be interested in tackling. On occasion, I've flirted with other ARM mini-PCs that are a little more expensive and a lot more powerful—like  Odroid XU4, or the newer  Odroid N2—but they still felt pretty constrained compared to even budget x86 PCs. The Odyssey seems tailor-made to address those performance concerns.
    (Ars Technica may earn compensation for sales from links on this post through  affiliate programs.)
 Odyssey's quad-core Celeron SoC might not be a powerhouse by desktop standards—but it's more than powerful enough to run a full Windows 10 desktop experience. Add in 8GiB of RAM, 64GB eMMC storage, one SATA-III port, two 1Gbps Ethernet jacks, dual M.2 slots (one B-key and one M-key), Intel 9560 Wi-Fi, Intel UHD 600 graphics and a full-size HDMI port, and it's hard to figure out what this $260 box  can't do.
 If you're looking to control other hardware on a very low level, Odyssey also has a Raspberry Pi-compatible 40-pin GPIO header and a 28-pin header for its ATSAMD21 Arduino coprocessor. We're not set up to test those functions, but Odyssey maker Seeed is also the manufacturer of the well-reputed  Grove sensor system—so when it tells us that the Odyssey's connectors and coprocessor are Grove-compatible, we're inclined to believe them.
 When it comes to form factor, the Odyssey in its re_computer case reminds us most of an unusually geeky Chromebox. Like the Chromebox, Odyssey in the re_computer case is just larger than the VESA mounting plate on the back of a monitor—and also like the Chromebox, it has VESA compatible mounting holes on the back. You'll need to provide your own mounting studs if you want to take advantage of that option, though.
 The re_computer case was frankly a little bit of a pain to assemble—the provided instructions consist of unlabeled diagrams only, and the diagrams aren't all accurate. In particular, we wished that they'd told us up front that the clear top lid of the re_computer was magnetically attached! The box lists the lid in the inventory as a separate part, but it's already snapped into the case itself, and it's not immediately clear that you can pry it loose easily with a spudger or other fine-edged tool.
 Once you actually get the re_computer case assembled, it's extremely attractive and functional. Any of the parts you need to mess with can be accessed by removing the magnetically attached clear lid, and the external ports are all easy to get to and unobscured. We also really like the cheerful royal blue that the sides of the case are anodized with.
     Odyssey's punchy Celeron J4105 processor is backed by plenty of RAM, storage, network, and graphics capabilities. Whether you prefer to run Windows or Linux, it can handle tasks that the CPU- and IO-limited Raspberry Pi series struggles with. If you want to build a high-end DIY router, it has dual gigabit Ethernet—and the J4105 CPU is significantly more powerful than the 1037U in our own  Homebrew router.
 If you're looking to build a  Kodi or similar home theater PC system, the J4105 and its UHD 600 graphics are more than up to the task, at least up to 1080P—4K is passable, but some videos will exhibit a little frame drop. In our testing, 4K videos on Vimeo played flawlessly; 4K on YouTube was watchable but noticeably dropped a frame here and there.
 You can even make a miniature fileserver out of the Odyssey. It offers one M.2 PCIe 2.0 x4 slot that can accommodate a high-performance NVMe SSD and one full-size SATA-III connector that can be connected to any standard SATA drive. Add that full-speed, reliable connectivity to the 8GiB onboard RAM, and you've got enough machine to run FreeNAS, XigmaNAS, or the upcoming  TrueNAS Core.
 Finally, you can just make a very usable desktop computer out of it. The version we tested came pre-installed with a fully activated Windows 10 Enterprise—it can be run by itself or joined to a domain. You can also buy the Odyssey without the Windows 10 license, if you prefer a Linux desktop.
 If whatever use case you've landed on needs cellular connectivity, Odyssey has you covered there as well—its M.2 B-keyed port can accommodate an  LTE module (not included), and there's a SIM card socket as well.
     The 8GiB of RAM and quad-core Celeron J4105 in the Odyssey, along with its Sandisk 64GB eMMC, are enough to make the little box feel like a real computer, even when running Windows. Our review sample came pre-installed and pre-activated with Windows 10 Enterprise, and it typically cold-booted fully to the desktop—including POST time—in 15-30 seconds. Once there, it felt perfectly responsive, either when installing applications or browsing the Web using Microsoft Edge.
 The little system shipped with Windows 10 build 1903, so the first thing we did was upgrade it to 2004. The only non-stock software installed on the system is an Arduino software package, so we didn't take before-and-after  Wiztree disk usage graphs this time around—it's a standard loadout of Windows, and it fits just fine on the onboard 64GB eMMC, both before and after the upgrade.
 We tested the Odyssey against our own original gangster  Homebrew Router from 2016, this year's  Kano PC, and Walmart's cursed  EVOO EV-C-116-5 laptop. The Odyssey, OG Homebrew, and Kano PC are all Celeron-based low-power systems; the AMD A4-9120 powered EVOO should be in the same class but isn't, due to its massive factory underclocking.
 When we thoroughly tested routing performance with challenging, small-packet workloads back in 2016, we found that the Celeron 1037U in the Homebrew was more than sufficient to sling packets at a full 1Gbps on a bare Ubuntu installation. When we tested pfSense, the more user-friendly and full-featured router distribution didn't do so well.
 Although Kano PC's N4000 is four years newer than the Homebrew's 1037U, with double the cores and threads, it isn't significantly faster. Odyssey's J4105 is a powerhouse by comparison—it's overwhelmingly faster than either in multi-threaded tests, and a little faster single-threaded to boot.
 Although we haven't directly tested the Odyssey as a dedicated router, its benchmarks lead us to believe it would do very well. We suspect that even when running pfSense or  opnSense, it should hang pretty well with the original Homebrew running vanilla Ubuntu.
  We're pretty excited about the Odyssey X86J4105. It scratches all the right itches for just about any maker or IoT project we can think of—and it brings tremendously more firepower to the table than ARM-based devices like the Raspberry Pi series, or even the Pi's more powerful Odroid competitors.
 If you're looking for the cheapest possible solution, the Odyssey may not be your cup of tea—but if you don't mind spending a little more, the flexibility offered by its full x86 architecture and staggering array of connectivity is well worth the extra money. That standard x86 architecture also makes it possible to leave the world of custom, hardware-focused system images—instead, you can just install Windows, Linux, or BSD directly onto the hardware from standard ISOs.
 The Odyssey could also make a great kid, kiosk, or even light office PC. It's small, it's extremely inexpensive for a fully activated Windows system, and its desktop experience is good—much better than Kano PC's or a typical low-end Chromebook's. If you opt for the 64GB eMMC storage, it's both fast enough and large enough for comfortable use with Windows 10—or if you want higher performance and more storage, you can use a standard M.2 PCIe or SATA SSD instead.
 The only complaint we have about the Odyssey is just how frustrating we found it to get the included Wi-Fi antennas connected to its Intel 9560 chipset. The diagrams don't properly label where the chipset actually is on the board, and the connector location made connecting the leads even more frustrating than it is on a standard laptop. We needed a jeweler's loupe and about ten straight minutes of careful, gentle fiddling and retrying before we finally got both leads properly connected.
title: Seed Studio的奥德赛是一款适用于大项目和小钱包的迷你PC
title_en: Seeed Studio’s Odyssey is a mini-PC for big projects and small wallets

note:
索尼周四发布了其最新的旗舰无线降噪耳机：WH-1000XM4。新的耳壳是索尼WH-1000XM3的后续产品，自2018年推出以来，WH-1000XM3一直被广泛认为是市场上最好的高级降噪耳机之一，也一直是ARS最喜欢的耳机之一。这款耳机是索尼WH-1000XM3的后续产品，自2018年推出以来，一直被广泛认为是市场上最好的高级降噪耳机之一。
据索尼称，XM4从今天开始接受预订，发货时间为8月中旬。这款耳机的售价为350美元，与发布时的XM3和Bose&39；的竞争对手QuietComfort 35 II的价格相同，但比Bose&#39；的降噪耳机700低50美元。
在过去的几天里，我手头有XM4；我计划在不久的将来进行更详细的比较，但现在我可以和今天的新闻分享一些初步的印象。
一般说来，XM4背后的思想更多的是一种改进，而不是彻底的重新发明。这个设计与它的前身几乎一模一样：我们仍然在寻找一个很漂亮的设计，塑料但坚固光滑的表面，有黑色或银色可供选择，带有铜色光泽。耳杯仍然可以折叠起来，便于存放，还有一个USB-C端口用于充电，而且在必要的情况下，该设备仍然可以通过附带的3.5毫米电缆工作。在右耳罩上仍然有一套基于触摸的控制，而不是物理的回放按钮。
不过，索尼表示，它在设计上做了一些轻微的调整，以适应更大的头部和耳朵尺寸：头带的衬垫略少，但色调也更宽，而耳垫的表面积据说增加了10%。舒适性并不是XM3普遍关注的问题，到目前为止，我还没有收到长时间佩戴XM4的重大投诉。它们仍然没有像百色的QuietComfort 35 II那样轻，但这里有足够的缓冲和空间，可以一次舒适地穿上几个小时。与此同时，它们仍然提供了一个刚刚牢固的密封，以防止太多的外部噪音渗入。
尽管XM3的有源消噪(ANC)功能接近同类最佳，但索尼表示，它已经改进了XM4的技术。该公司声称，这款耳机使用了新的蓝牙音频SoC(该公司没有具体说明是哪种)，并为其自己的QN1噪声消除芯片使用了更新的算法，以实时更新和处理噪声消除。根据索尼的说法，这应该会使XM4在抑制低端频率(如机舱内或隆隆作响的火车上)方面略有改善，但在中高端频率(大多数人类声音和日常噪音驻留的地方)也会提供更明显的增益。
我还没能对这一说法进行真正的压力测试--全球大流行并不是解决此类问题的最佳环境--但我可以说，这里的非国大质量仍然令人印象深刻，即使没有播放任何音乐，也几乎完全消除了忙碌的空调发出的嗡嗡声。如果没有其他因素，它似乎并没有比以前降级。没有一款降噪耳机可以真正让一切变得静音，这一点在这里不会改变，但在否定这些高端噪音方面取得的任何进展都将是一件好事。
除此之外，XM4仍然具有环境音效模式，可以很好地融入外部噪音，而不会完全淹没你的音乐，以防你想更好地感知周围环境。它的强度仍然可以在索尼的配套应用程序中找到的20分刻度范围内进行调节，而且该模式仍然可以通过耳机本身的一个按钮来激活。在XM3上，该按钮只需在ANC、环境声音和标准音频模式之间切换。在这里，它被一个自定义按钮取代，该按钮还可以设置为激活谷歌助手或Alexa语音助手，这两个按钮都有内置支持。不过，仍然没有手动调整ANC强度的选项，这是Bose的降噪耳机700提供的一个关键功能。
也许到目前为止，我使用过的最有用的新功能是索尼所谓的“说话聊天”功能。在索尼的配套应用程序中对其进行设置后，该功能可以让XM4在检测到你的声音后自动暂停你的音乐，并放大环境声音设置。对于那些当你正在做某事时出现的简短对话来说，这非常方便--你不需要摘下耳机，也不需要笨手笨脚地按任何暂停按钮，你只需在需要的时候说话，然后双击正确的耳罩，或者等待15秒，就可以开始播放你正在听的任何东西。这项功能可以通过配套的应用程序随时打开和关闭，也可以通过将手放在耳杯上几秒钟来打开和关闭。
这还不包括XM3的快速注意模式，该模式允许你将手放在耳罩上，暂时降低音量，并让周围的噪音进入，比如空乘通知。
索尼还推出了一项新的自适应声音控制(Adaptive Sound Control)功能，该功能更侧重于位置跟踪和智能耳机功能。这是索尼的配套应用程序中控制的另一个设置。本质上，它允许您根据您的活动级别和当前位置编程不同的音频设置-环境声音强度、EQ设置、ANC和聊天激活，后者是XM4中的新功能。例如，你可以设置耳机，一旦它识别出你正在坐下，它就会自动将所有声音静音，或者当你在星巴克附近想要拿起一杯咖啡而不想暂停音乐时，你可以让耳机调高环境音量，打开语音聊天。你可以在应用程序中手动标记不同预置的位置，索尼表示，随着时间的推移，耳机将自动识别经常访问的位置。谢天谢地，对于那些不想让耳机不断追踪他们的位置的人来说，所有这些都是完全可选的。但它是为那些想要更免提的旅行耳机体验的人准备的。
索尼并没有用XM4宣传音频质量的巨大变化。主要吹捧的功能是XM3上发现的DSEE HX音频升级软件的更新版；在这里，这项技术被称为DSEE Extreme，索尼表示，它将在实时恢复压缩数字文件中丢失的音频数据方面更加准确。这款耳机还支持索尼新兴的空间音频技术--它能让声音听起来像是从你周围传来的声音，就像模拟的扬声器系统一样--不过只有几个流媒体服务支持这一功能，而且混合格式的曲目库也相对较小。
根据我早先的听力，XM4仍然有一种适度的低音，低音强劲，有时会在高音中比细节更耀眼。在高音中，XM4的声音仍然是适度的，低音强劲，有时可以超过高音中的细节。就形象和细节而言，它并不是世界一流的--非国大仍然是这里的主要卖点--但它绝对不错，很少听起来刺耳，它应该特别吸引那些喜欢多一点低音的人。不过，如果你不喜欢默认调谐，你仍然可以切换几个不同的EQ预设，或者使用索尼配套应用程序中功能适中的EQ工具自己定制声音。那真是太棒了。
值得注意的是，索尼已经不再支持高通(Qualcomm&#39；)的aptX和aptX高清蓝牙音频编解码器。相反，XM4只支持SBC、AAC和索尼自己的LDAC。对于包括所有iPhone用户在内的大多数用户来说，这不会有太大的不同：苹果从来没有在iOS设备上支持过aptX，而且大多数人感觉不到aptX和基本的SBC编解码器之间的音频质量差异。但是，有了这样一副高端耳机，aptX HD的改进可能会更明显一些，虽然LDAC的音质上限更高，但它需要更多的带宽才能到达那里，这可能会导致你在移动时连接起来很麻烦。不过，LDAC在稳定的环境中也有它的好处，而且现在许多Android手机上都可以使用LDAC。然而，它不能在iOS设备上使用。
索尼对XM4的电池续航时间进行了评级，在ANC激活的情况下续航时间为30小时，在没有ANC的情况下续航时间长达38小时，这与XM3的续航时间相同。你实际得到的结果将取决于你演奏的声音有多大，但无论是哪种方式，你都应该得到足够的声音来听几天。该公司表示，10分钟的充电应该会导致大约5个小时的播放时间。该设备有五个内置麦克风，索尼正在兜售改善的通话质量，这是Bose目前拥有明显优势的一个领域。
整个设备都是通过蓝牙5连接的；到目前为止，我还没有遇到任何连接问题，每次配对完成后，耳机都会自动重新连接到我的Google Pixel3a上
XM4的另一个新功能是佩戴检测功能--与苹果AirPods等其他耳机非常相似，XM4有一个内置的运动传感器，可以自动检测耳机何时戴上和摘下，并相应地暂停和播放音乐。
在做出任何明确的判断之前，我将不得不花更多的时间来研究XM4，但总的来说，索尼似乎并没有改变XM3如此值得称赞的核心：强大的ANC和舒适的设计中良好的音效。并不是所有较新的智能功能都很诱人，但像语音聊天、多设备连接和动作检测等功能都有立竿见影的效果。虽然你必须花大量时间在这款配套应用上，才能让它们发挥最大作用，但XM4看起来似乎会成为高端无线降噪耳机市场上的竞争者。
note_en:
Sony on Thursday announced its latest flagship pair of wireless noise-cancelling headphones: the WH-1000XM4. The new over-ear cans are the follow-up to Sony's WH-1000XM3, which have been widely regarded as being among the best premium pairs of noise-cancelling headphones on the market—and  have been an  Ars favorite—since launching in 2018.
 The XM4 is  available for pre-order starting today, with shipping to start sometime in "mid-August," according to Sony. The headphones cost $350, which is the same price as the XM3 at launch and  Bose's competing QuietComfort 35 II, but $50 less than  Bose's Noise Cancelling Headphones 700.
 I've had the XM4 on hand for the past couple of days; I plan to have a more detailed comparison in the near future, but for now I can share some initial impressions alongside today's news.
  In general, the idea behind the XM4 is one of refinement more than radical reinvention. The design is nearly identical to its predecessor: we're still looking at a largely handsome design with a plastic yet sturdy and smooth finish, which is available in black or silver with copper accents. The earcups still fold up for easy storage, there's still a USB-C port for charging, and the device can still work over an included 3.5mm cable in a pinch. There's still a set of touch-based controls on the right earcup instead of physical playback buttons.
 Sony says it has made some slight design tweaks to accommodate more head and ear sizes, though: the headband is slightly less padded but also a tinge wider, while the earpads are said to have a 10-percent larger surface area. Comfort was not a general area of concern with the XM3, and so far I've had no major complaints wearing the XM4 for extended periods. They still aren't as light as something like Bose's QuietComfort 35 II, but there's enough cushioning and space here to feel agreeable for hours at a time. At the same time, they still provide a just-firm-enough seal to keep too much outside noise from seeping in.
  Though the active noise cancellation (ANC) on the XM3 was close to best in class, Sony says it has improved upon the tech with the XM4. The company claims that the headphones use a new Bluetooth audio SoC—it didn't specify which—and an updated algorithm for its own QN1 noise-cancelling chip to update and process noise cancelling in real time. According to Sony, this should make the XM4 slightly better at muting out low-end frequencies such as in airplane cabins or on rumbling trains but also provide more noticeable gains in mid- and higher-end frequencies, where most human voices and everyday noise reside.
 I haven't been able to truly stress test this claim yet—a global pandemic isn't the most accommodating setting for such matters—but I can say that the ANC quality here is still deeply impressive, almost totally wiping out the droning of a busy air conditioner even without any music playing. If nothing else, it doesn't appear to be a downgrade from before. No noise-cancelling headphone can truly mute  everything, and that won't change here, but any progress toward negating those higher-end noises would be a good thing.
 Beyond that, the XM4 still features an "ambient sound" mode that does a reasonably good job of blending in outside noise without overwhelming your music completely, just in case you want to be better aware of your surroundings. Its strength is still adjustable on a 20-point scale found within Sony's companion app, and the mode can still be activated through a button on the headphones themselves. On the XM3, that button simply swapped between ANC, ambient sound, and a standard audio mode. Here, it has been replaced with a "Custom" button that can also be set to activate either the Google Assistant or Alexa voice assistant, both of which have built-in support. There's still no option to manually adjust the strength of the ANC, though, which is a key feature offered by Bose's Noise Cancelling Headphones 700.
 Perhaps the most useful new feature I've used thus far is something Sony calls "Speak to Chat." After setting it up in Sony's companion app, the feature lets the XM4 automatically pause your music and amp up the ambient sound setting once it detects your voice. This is highly convenient for those brief conversations that come up when you're in the middle of something—instead of having to take the headphones off or fumbling with any pause buttons, you can simply speak when you need to, then either double-tap the right earcup or wait 15 seconds to start back up whatever you were listening to. The feature can be turned on and off at any time through the companion app or by holding your hand over the earcup for several seconds.
 That comes in addition to "Quick Attention" mode found on the XM3, which lets you hold your hand over that earcup to momentarily lower the volume and let in ambient noise for things like flight attendant announcements.
 Sony has also introduced a new "Adaptive Sound Control" feature that leans more heavily into location tracking and "smart headphone" functionality. This is another setting controlled in Sony's companion app. Essentially, it lets you program different audio settings—ambient sound strength, EQ settings, ANC and Speak to Chat activation—based on your activity level and current location, the latter being new to the XM4. You could set the headphones to automatically mute everything once it recognizes you're sitting down, for instance, or have it ramp up the ambient sound and turn on Speak to Chat when you're near a Starbucks and want to pick up a coffee without pausing your music. You can manually mark locations for different pre-sets within the app, and Sony says the headphones will automatically recognize frequently visited locations over time. Thankfully, all of this is completely optional for those who don't want their headphones constantly tracking their location. But it's there for those who want a more hands-free experience with headphones designed for travel.
  Sony isn't advertising massive changes to audio quality with the XM4. The main touted feature is an updated version of the  DSEE HX audio upscaling software found on the XM3; here, the tech is called "DSEE Extreme," and Sony says it will be more accurate at "restoring" audio data lost in compressed digital files in real time. The headphones also support Sony's burgeoning " 360 Reality Audio" spatial audio tech—which essentially makes audio sound like it's coming from all around you, as in a simulated speaker system—though only a few streaming services like Tidal and Deezer support the feature, and the library of tracks that have been mixed in the format is relatively small.
 Based on my early listening, the XM4 still has a moderately bass-forward sound, with a punchy low end that can sometimes outshine details in the treble. It's not a world-beater in terms of imaging and detail—the ANC is still the main selling point here—but it's definitely good, it rarely sounds harsh, and it should particularly appeal to those who like a little extra bass. If you don't like the default tuning, though, you can still swap through a few different EQ pre-sets or customize the sound yourself with the moderately powerful EQ tool in Sony's companion app. That's great.
 One enthusiast tidbit to note is that Sony has dropped support for Qualcomm's aptX and aptX HD Bluetooth audio codecs. Instead, the XM4 only supports SBC, AAC, and  Sony's own LDAC. For most users, including all iPhone owners, this won't make much of a difference: Apple has never supported aptX on iOS devices, and the difference in audio quality between aptX and the base SBC codec  isn't perceptible to most. But aptX HD's improvements can be a little more noticeable with a pair of high-end headphones like this, and while LDAC has a higher audio quality ceiling, it requires more bandwidth to get there, which can result in a finicky connection when you're on the move. LDAC has its benefits in a stable environment, though, and it's available on many Android phones these days. It's not available on iOS devices, however.
 Sony rates the XM4's battery life at 30 hours with ANC activated and up to 38 hours without, which is the same as the XM3. What you actually get will depend on how loudly you play things, but you should get enough for a few days of listening either way. The company says a 10-minute charge should result in roughly five hours of playback time. The device has five built-in microphones, and Sony is touting improved call quality, which is an area where Bose currently holds a distinct advantage.
 The whole thing connects over Bluetooth 5; so far I've had no issues with connection hiccups, and the headphones have automatically reconnected to my Google Pixel 3a each time after the initial pairing process. Notably, the XM4 will have the ability to connect to two devices over Bluetooth simultaneously. Sony hasn't fully set this feature live yet, so I haven't been able to test its specifics, but the company says it will work through the aforementioned companion app and become available through a firmware update around the device's launch.
 Also new is a "wearing detection" feature—much like other headphones such as Apple's AirPods, the XM4 have a built-in motion sensor that can automatically detect when the headphones have been put on and taken off, pausing and playing music accordingly.
 I'll have to spend more time with the XM4 before making any definitive judgments, but generally speaking, Sony seemingly hasn't changed the core of what made the XM3 such a laudable device: strong ANC and good sound in a comfortable design. Not all of the newer "smart" features are enticing, but things like Speak to Chat, multi-device connectivity, and motion detection have immediate benefits. While you'll have to spend a good chunk of time in the companion app to get the most out of them, the XM4 look as if they'll be a contender for those in the market for a premium pair of wireless noise-cancelling headphones.
title: 索尼推出新款WH-1000XM4降噪耳机与Bose展开竞争
title_en: Sony takes on Bose with new WH-1000XM4 noise-cancelling headphones

note:
TikTok上一个拥有大量粉丝的保守派账号上传了一段视频，告诉民主党人在选举日的第二天投票，这违反了竞选平台针对选举错误信息的规定。这段视频似乎是在平台上发布了一个模因，告诉人们在错误的日期投票，而这是TikTok规则所禁止的。
共和党的炒作之家是TikTok上广受关注的保守派“炒作之家”账号之一，“纽约时报”将其描述为“短小的电视网，每个电视网都有一群会说话的人”。最近，该公司上传了一段名为“突发新闻”的视频，视频中一人谎称，由于冠状病毒大流行，“选举日被分成了两个不同的日子。”这位人士告诉共和党人和无党派人士“11月3日去投票”--指的是实际的选举日--而“民主党人和其他政党附属机构，坚持住，然后确保在11月4日去投票。”这段视频最初是由另一个账号在7月份上传的，该账号隶属于共和党的炒作众议院，并表达了对QAnon阴谋论的支持，目前已有超过4万人次观看。
最近几周，多名TikTok用户也上传了视频，告诉共和党人在11月4日投票。(Media Matters不会显示未成年人创建的视频的身份识别信息，以保护他们的身份。)。
TikTok的社区指南明确禁止这种错误信息：在其“误导性信息”部分，该平台禁止“在选举或其他公民程序方面误导社区成员的内容”。
共和党大肆炒作众议院视频的同时，另一个在TikTok上拥有大量粉丝的保守派账号@save ativehypehouse上传了一段经过欺骗性编辑的2020年民主党候选人、前副总统乔·拜登(Joe Biden)的视频，点击量超过25万次。几个月前，TikTok曾告诉Business Insider，编辑后的拜登视频违反了其关于误导性信息的规定，而在其重新传播之际，TikTok扩大了规则，明确禁止“通过以可能造成伤害的方式歪曲事件真相，操纵误导用户的内容”。尽管违反了TikTok的指导方针，但该视频在最终被删除之前，仍在平台上保留了几天。
note_en:
A conservative account on TikTok with a large following uploaded a video telling Democrats to vote the day after Election Day, violating the platform’s rules against election misinformation. The video appears to have spread following a meme on the platform telling people to vote on the wrong day, which is prohibited by TikTok’s rules.
 The Republican Hype House, one of the widely followed conservative “hype house” accounts on TikTok that  The New York Times has described as “short-form TV networks, each with a cast of talking heads,” recently uploaded a video labeled “Breaking News” featuring a person falsely claiming that due to the novel coronavirus pandemic, “Election Day has been split into two separate days.” The person told Republicans and independents to “head to the polls on November 3” -- referring to the actual Election Day -- while “Democrats and other party affiliates, hang tight and then make sure to head to the polls on November 4.” The video, which was  originally uploaded in July by another account that  affiliates itself with the Republican Hype House and has  expressed support for the  QAnon conspiracy theory, has more than 40,000 views.
    Multiple  TikTok users in recent weeks have also  uploaded videos telling Republicans to vote on November 4. (Media Matters is not showing identifying information for videos created by minors to protect their identities.)
 TikTok’s  Community Guidelines clearly prohibit this misinformation: In its section on “misleading information,” the platform bars “content that misleads community members about elections or other civic processes.”
 The Republican Hype House video also comes right around the same time as  another conservative account with a major following on TikTok, @conservativehypehouse,  uploaded a deceptively edited clip of 2020 Democratic candidate and former Vice President Joe Biden that received more than 250,000 views. TikTok had  told Business Insider months earlier that the edited video of Biden violated its rules on misleading information, and its renewed spread comes as TikTok has  expanded its rules to specifically prohibit “manipulated content that misleads users by distorting the truth of events in a way that could cause harm.” That video also remained up on the platform for days, despite violating TikTok’s guidelines, before it was finally taken down.
title: TikTok的一段视频告诉民主党人在选举日之后投票，违反了平台的规定
title_en: A TikTok video told Democrats to vote after Election Day, violating the platform’s rules
